title: Medivators DSD-201 / DSD Edge Series Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Cycle Not Starting or Aborting Mid-Cycle
description: Troubleshooting cycle start failures or mid-cycle aborts caused by door, water, chemical, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on a failed or incomplete reprocessing cycle for patient use. Remove any scopes involved from service until properly reprocessed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to start a cycle and observe if it fails immediately or mid-process, check for alarms, and identify the stage where the cycle stops.

steps.title: Check Door Closure and Latch Status (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Open and firmly close the door, inspect gasket condition, ensure latch engages, and check for door-related alarms.

steps.title: Verify Water Supply and Pressure
steps.instructions: Confirm water supply is on, inspect hoses and filters, and observe for fill delays or alarms.

steps.title: Check Chemical Levels and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure disinfectant and detergent levels are adequate, pickup tubes are inserted, and no dosing alarms are present.

steps.title: Inspect Scope Connections and Channel Hookups
steps.instructions: Verify proper scope connections, correct configuration, and check for loose tubing.

steps.title: Check for Leak Test Failures
steps.instructions: Observe leak test phase, inspect for leaks, and test with a known good scope if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Drain and Waste Line
steps.instructions: Check for kinks or blockages in drain hose and confirm proper facility drainage.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 30–60 seconds, restart, and attempt cycle again.

CCR.Complaint: AER cycle would not start and intermittently aborted during operation.
CCR.Cause: Door latch not fully engaging, preventing system from recognizing sealed condition.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted door closure and verified proper latch engagement; unit tested and passed full cycle.

title: Medivators DSD-201 / DSD Edge Series Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Leak Test Failure Preventing Cycle Initiation
description: Leak test failures that prevent cycle start due to scope connection issues, damaged channels, or improper setup.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure no scope in use is needed for an active procedure. Remove the scope from clinical use before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt to start a cycle and observe the leak test failure message. Confirm leak test fails immediately or during pressurization. Note any error codes or messages.

steps.title: Check Scope Connection to AER (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Inspect how the endoscope is connected to the AER ports. Ensure all channel connectors are properly attached. Verify correct hookup for the specific scope type. Disconnect and reconnect all fittings securely.

steps.title: Inspect Leak Test Tubing and Adapters
steps.instructions: Check all external tubing used for leak testing. Inspect tubing for cracks, kinks, or disconnections. Verify adapters are correct and not damaged. Swap with known good tubing/adapters if available.

steps.title: Check the Endoscope for External Damage or Moisture
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the scope prior to testing. Look for visible damage, cracks, or worn insertion tube. Check for fluid intrusion or residual moisture. Ensure scope was properly pre-cleaned and dried.

steps.title: Verify Proper Channel Hookup Configuration
steps.instructions: Ensure all required channels are connected correctly. Confirm all channels required for the model scope are connected. Ensure unused ports are properly capped if required.

steps.title: Check Door Seal and Latch Integrity
steps.instructions: Inspect the AER door and sealing surface. Ensure door is fully closed and latched. Inspect gasket for damage, debris, or wear. Clean gasket if contaminated.

steps.title: Restart the AER System
steps.instructions: Perform a system reset. Power cycle the unit. Restart and attempt leak test again.

CCR.Complaint: AER cycle would not start due to repeated leak test failure.
CCR.Cause: Loose channel connection at scope interface causing pressure loss during leak test.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected all scope channels securely and verified successful leak test and cycle initiation.

helpfulDetails: Leak test behavior (immediate vs gradual failure)
Scope type and channel configuration used
Any visible scope damage or moisture
Final device status (passed leak test or removed from service)

title: Medivators DSD-201 / DSD Edge Series Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Chemical Dosing or Disinfectant Level Errors
description: Errors related to low disinfectant levels, improper chemical dosing, or failure to initiate/complete cycles due to chemical system issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot during an active reprocessing cycle with a scope inside. Remove any scope and use a backup AER if needed. Expected: No patient care delay or risk of improperly disinfected equipment

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact error message on the display. Note whether it indicates low disinfectant, dosing failure, or chemical alarm. Determine if the issue occurs at cycle start or mid-cycle. Expected: Clear understanding of when and how the error occurs. Why it matters: Helps differentiate between supply issue vs dosing system fault

steps.title: Check Disinfectant Level (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Inspect the disinfectant container or reservoir. Verify container is not empty or below minimum level. Ensure correct chemical is being used. Replace with a full, approved disinfectant container if low. Expected: Proper fluid level restores normal operation. Why it matters: Low chemical levels will prevent cycle initiation or trigger alarms

steps.title: Verify Chemical Container Installation
steps.instructions: Ensure the disinfectant container is properly installed. Confirm container is seated correctly in its holder. Check that any caps, seals, or connectors are properly engaged. Reseat the container if needed. Expected: Secure connection with no looseness or misalignment. Why it matters: Improper seating prevents correct chemical draw

steps.title: Inspect Chemical Pickup Lines and Tubing
steps.instructions: Check for issues with chemical delivery lines. Inspect tubing for kinks, cracks, or disconnections. Ensure pickup tube is fully submerged in disinfectant. Look for air bubbles in lines (if visible). Expected: Continuous, unobstructed tubing path. Why it matters: Air or blockage prevents accurate dosing

steps.title: Check for Empty or Expired Chemical Indicators
steps.instructions: Review system prompts or indicators related to chemical status. Confirm chemical has not reached maximum reuse cycles (if applicable). Replace chemical if system indicates expiration or reuse limit reached. Expected: System accepts new chemical and clears error. Why it matters: Many AERs track chemical life and will lock out expired solutions

steps.title: Inspect Filters and Water Supply (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Some dosing errors are linked to flow issues. Verify water supply is on and within normal pressure. Check inlet filters for blockage. Expected: Normal flow supports proper chemical mixing and dosing. Why it matters: Flow interruptions can trigger dosing or dilution errors

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Reset the AER after verifying all external conditions. Power cycle the unit. Attempt to start a new cycle. Expected: Error clears if caused by temporary fault. Why it matters: Clears minor sensor or logic errors

CCR.Complaint: AER would not start cycle and displayed chemical dosing error.
CCR.Cause: Disinfectant container was low and pickup tube was not fully submerged.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced disinfectant container and reseated pickup tubing; unit passed test cycle.

title: Medivators DSD-201 / DSD Edge Series Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Channel Blockage or Flow Error Alarms
description: Troubleshooting channel blockage or flow-related alarms caused by scope connections, tubing issues, filters, or flow restrictions preventing proper reprocessing.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot with a contaminated or patient-used scope connected. Remove the scope and follow proper infection control procedures before continuing.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Note if alarm occurs during fill, wash, or rinse phase. Check if the cycle stops immediately or attempts to continue.

steps.title: Check Scope Channel Connections (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify all scope channel connectors are attached correctly. Ensure correct port-to-channel mapping. Disconnect and firmly reconnect all lines.

steps.title: Inspect Channel Tubing for Kinks or Occlusions
steps.instructions: Inspect all tubing for bends, kinks, or compression. Ensure tubing is not pinched behind the unit or door. Replace damaged tubing if needed.

steps.title: Check for Internal or External Blockage in the Scope
steps.instructions: Verify the scope was properly pre-cleaned. Flush channels manually if appropriate. Substitute with a known good scope to compare behavior.

steps.title: Inspect Water Supply and Pressure
steps.instructions: Verify water supply valves are fully open. Check inlet hoses for kinks or restrictions. Confirm adequate facility water pressure.

steps.title: Check Filters and Strainers
steps.instructions: Inspect inlet water filters and internal strainers (if accessible). Clean or replace filters per facility protocol.

steps.title: Inspect Drain Line for Restrictions
steps.instructions: Ensure drain hose is not kinked or elevated improperly. Check for slow drainage or backup. Verify facility drain is clear.

steps.title: Restart Cycle After Corrections
steps.instructions: Run a cycle without a scope (if allowed). Then test with a known good scope.

CCR.Complaint: AER displaying channel blockage alarm and stopping mid-cycle during reprocessing.
CCR.Cause: Kinked channel tubing restricting flow to scope lumen.
CCR.Resolution: Tubing repositioned and verified unobstructed; system tested and returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Alarm message text and cycle phase
Scope type and channel configuration used
Test results with known good scope

title: Medivators DSD-201 / DSD Edge Series Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Door Not Sealing or Latch Failure
description: Troubleshooting door seal or latch failures preventing cycle start, causing leaks, alarms, or inability to maintain proper pressure during reprocessing.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not attempt troubleshooting during an active reprocessing cycle with contaminated scopes inside. Remove scopes if safe to do so and follow infection control protocol.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue and Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt to close and latch the door while observing system response. Determine if the door fails to latch, if the system alarms or refuses to start, and if there are visible gaps or resistance when closing.

steps.title: Inspect Door for Obstructions (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Remove any debris, tubing, or improperly routed scope lines. Ensure trays and scopes are properly seated and not interfering with the door.

steps.title: Check Door Gasket / Seal Condition
steps.instructions: Look for cracks, flattening, or buildup of residue. Wipe gasket clean with approved materials.

steps.title: Verify Proper Door Alignment
steps.instructions: Close the door slowly and observe alignment. Look for uneven gaps or misalignment and gently apply pressure to see if latch engages.

steps.title: Check Latch Mechanism Operation
steps.instructions: Listen for latch engagement and check for loose or damaged latch components. Ensure handle moves freely if present.

steps.title: Inspect Door Sensor / Interlock Feedback
steps.instructions: Check for door closed indicator on the display. Open and close the door multiple times and look for intermittent detection.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Power off the unit, wait 30 seconds, then power back on and retry door closure.

CCR.Complaint: AER door would not latch; cycle would not start and door open alarm present.
CCR.Cause: Door gasket contaminated with residue and slight obstruction from improperly routed scope tubing preventing full closure.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction, cleaned gasket, verified proper door closure and successful cycle initiation.

helpfulDetails: Presence of obstruction or misloaded scopes

title: Medivators Advantage Plus Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Cycle Not Starting or Aborting Mid-Cycle
description: Cycle fails to start or stops mid-process due to door, setup, water, chemical, or connection issues.

steps.title: 1. Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Do not attempt to troubleshoot while a scope is needed for patient care. Remove the AER from use if a cycle cannot complete. Expected: No impact to patient care; alternate reprocessing method in use. Why it matters: Incomplete disinfection presents infection risk.

steps.title: 2. Verify the Reported Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm if the cycle will not start at all or starts and aborts mid-cycle. Action: Attempt to start a cycle; Note any error messages or alarms; Identify when the failure occurs (start vs during wash/disinfect/dry phase). Expected: Behavior is repeatable or clearly observed. Why it matters: Helps narrow the failure stage and likely cause.

steps.title: 3. Check Door Closure and Seal (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: AER will not run if the door is not fully sealed. Action: Open and firmly re-close the door; Inspect gasket for debris, damage, or misalignment; Ensure latch fully engages. Expected: Door locks securely and unit allows cycle start. Why it matters: Safety interlock prevents operation if chamber is not sealed.

steps.title: 4. Verify Scope Connections and Channel Hookups
steps.instructions: Improper or incomplete connections can prevent cycle initiation or cause aborts. Action: Confirm all scope channels are connected correctly; Ensure connectors are seated and not loose; Check for kinked or disconnected tubing. Expected: All connections are secure and recognized. Why it matters: Flow or leak detection failures will stop the cycle.

steps.title: 5. Check Water Supply and Pressure
steps.instructions: Insufficient or interrupted water supply can stop the cycle. Action: Verify facility water supply valves are open; Check for adequate pressure and flow; Inspect inlet hoses for kinks or restrictions. Expected: Stable water supply with no alarms. Why it matters: AER cycles rely on consistent water flow for all phases.

steps.title: 6. Verify Chemical Levels and Containers
steps.instructions: Low or improperly seated chemical containers can prevent or abort cycles. Action: Check disinfectant and detergent levels; Ensure containers are properly installed; Inspect pickup tubes for proper placement. Expected: Adequate chemical levels and proper detection. Why it matters: System will not proceed without confirmed chemical availability.

steps.title: 7. Inspect Filters and Drain Path
steps.instructions: Blocked filters or drains can cause flow errors and abort cycles. Action: Check and clean inlet filters if accessible; Inspect chamber drain for blockage; Ensure no standing water remains after previous cycle. Expected: Free-flowing drainage and no obstructions. Why it matters: Flow restrictions trigger safety shutdowns.

steps.title: 8. Perform a Power Cycle (Reset Attempt)
steps.instructions: Clears temporary faults or control glitches. Action: Turn unit OFF; Wait 30–60 seconds; Power back ON and attempt cycle again. Expected: Cycle starts normally if issue was transient. Why it matters: Resets system control logic.

steps.title: 9. Observe for Specific Error Codes or Patterns
steps.instructions: If the issue persists, note any consistent alarms or failure points. Action: Record exact error messages; Identify phase where abort occurs. Expected: Consistent error behavior identified. Why it matters: Provides direction for further diagnosis or service.

CCR.Complaint: AER cycle not starting and intermittently aborting during disinfection phase.
CCR.Cause: Door not fully sealing due to debris on gasket causing interlock failure.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned gasket, verified proper door seal, and confirmed successful cycle completion.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested and unit powered normally
Door latch and seal condition verified
Alarm behavior and when the abort occurs
Accessories swapped or connection tubing replaced
Environmental factors such as water pressure, drain backup, or chemical storage issue
Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: Medivators Advantage Plus Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Leak Test Failure Preventing Cycle Initiation
description: Leak test failures preventing cycle initiation due to connection issues, scope leaks, or improper setup.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot with a scope intended for patient use. Remove scope and transition care to a properly processed scope.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt to start a cycle and observe when failure occurs, noting alarms or messages.

steps.title: Check Scope Connection Integrity
steps.instructions: Reconnect all scope channels, verify correct hookup, and ensure secure connections.

steps.title: Inspect the Endoscope for Damage
steps.instructions: Check for cracks, kinks, or prior leak test failures.

steps.title: Verify Correct Leak Test Adapter Use
steps.instructions: Ensure correct adapter is used and inspect seals or swap if needed.

steps.title: Check Tubing for Kinks or Blockage
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing and correct any restrictions.

steps.title: Perform Manual Leak Test
steps.instructions: Use manual tester to confirm scope integrity.

steps.title: Retry with Different Scope
steps.instructions: Test with known-good scope to isolate issue.

CCR.Complaint: AER fails leak test and will not start disinfection cycle.
CCR.Cause: Loose scope channel connection causing air leak during pressure test.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected scope properly and verified successful cycle initiation.

title: Medivators Advantage Plus Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Chemical Dosing or Disinfectant Level Errors
description: Addresses chemical dosing errors, low disinfectant alarms, or cycle failures related to improper disinfectant levels or delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not attempt to troubleshoot while a scope is needed for immediate patient use. Remove the AER from clinical workflow if cycles cannot complete. Expected: No impact to patient care; alternate reprocessing method in use. Why it matters: Incomplete disinfection creates infection risk.

steps.title: Verify the Error Message and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm or error on the display. Note the message (low disinfectant, dosing failure, or chemical error). Determine when it occurs (start of cycle, mid-cycle, or during fill phase). Expected: Error is consistent or reproducible. Why it matters: Identifies whether this is a supply issue vs system-controlled dosing failure.

steps.title: Check Disinfectant Level (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Inspect the disinfectant container level. Ensure it is not empty or below minimum level. Replace with a full, approved chemical if low. Expected: Proper fluid level restored and error clears. Why it matters: Low chemical supply is the most frequent cause of dosing errors.

steps.title: Verify Correct Chemical Type and Installation
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct disinfectant is installed for the system. Ensure container is properly seated and connected. Check that caps, seals, or pickup tubes are properly installed. Expected: System recognizes chemical correctly. Why it matters: Incorrect or improperly installed chemicals can prevent proper dosing.

steps.title: Inspect Chemical Pickup Tube and Connections
steps.instructions: Check tubing from the chemical container to the unit. Look for kinks, disconnections, or air gaps. Ensure pickup tube is fully submerged in the chemical. Expected: Continuous, unobstructed fluid path. Why it matters: Air in the line or blockage prevents accurate dosing.

steps.title: Check for Empty or Expired Chemical Container Sensors
steps.instructions: Inspect sensor indicators (if present) for chemical level detection. Ensure float or level sensors are not stuck or obstructed. Gently reseat container if needed. Expected: Sensor properly detects chemical presence. Why it matters: False “empty” readings will prevent dosing even if fluid is present.

steps.title: Run a Test Cycle or Prime Function
steps.instructions: Initiate a cycle or prime/flush function. Observe whether chemical is drawn into the system. Watch for alarms during dosing phase. Expected: Chemical is pulled and cycle progresses normally. Why it matters: Confirms whether the issue is resolved or persists during active operation.

steps.title: Check for External Leaks or Spills
steps.instructions: Inspect around chemical container, tubing, and internal chamber area. Look for signs of leaking disinfectant. Expected: No visible leaks. Why it matters: Leaks reduce available volume and may trigger dosing errors.

CCR.Complaint: AER displaying low disinfectant/dosing error and unable to complete cycle.
CCR.Cause: Disinfectant container empty causing dosing failure alarm.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced disinfectant container, verified proper installation, and confirmed successful cycle completion.

title: Medivators Advantage Plus Endoscope Reprocessor (AER) - Channel Blockage or Flow/Pressure Alarm
description: Flow or pressure alarms caused by blocked channels, improper scope connections, or water/air flow restrictions during reprocessing cycles.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while a scope is needed for a patient. Remove the AER from use if a cycle cannot be completed.

steps.title: Verify the Alarm and Cycle Stage
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm message and when it occurs (leak test, wash, rinse, or purge).

steps.title: Check Scope Connection and Channel Hookups (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect all channel lines, verify correct hookup configuration for the scope type, and ensure no loose or partially seated fittings.

steps.title: Inspect for Kinked or Damaged Tubing
steps.instructions: Check all external tubing for kinks, bends, or compression. Straighten any bent tubing and replace damaged lines.

steps.title: Check Scope Channels for Obstruction
steps.instructions: Remove the scope and manually flush channels using an approved device to observe for restricted or uneven flow.

steps.title: Verify Filters and Water Supply
steps.instructions: Confirm water supply is ON and within normal pressure and inspect and replace inlet filters if clogged.

steps.title: Inspect Internal AER Channel Connectors (External Access Only)
steps.instructions: Visually inspect accessible ports for residue and clean per manufacturer-approved methods without disassembly.

steps.title: Run a Cycle Without a Scope (Isolation Test)
steps.instructions: Operate the AER empty or with test fixtures to determine if the issue is scope-related or internal to the AER.

CCR.Complaint: AER alarming with flow/pressure error during wash cycle and aborting process.
CCR.Cause: Obstruction found in endoscope biopsy channel restricting flow.
CCR.Resolution: Flushed and cleared channel; verified normal operation with successful cycle completion.

title: Medivators Advantage Plus Automated Endoscope Reprocessor - Door Not Sealing or Latch Failure
description: Troubleshooting door sealing and latch failures preventing cycle start due to interlock or alignment issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not attempt to run or troubleshoot the AER while it is processing a scope. Remove the scope and ensure no active cycle is in progress.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue and Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt to close the door and start a cycle. Observe if the door physically closes, if the latch engages or fails, and check for any alarms or error messages.

steps.title: Inspect Door Alignment and Obstructions (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Check for debris, dried disinfectant, residue, misaligned racks, or foreign objects. Clean residue and reposition trays as needed.

steps.title: Check Door Gasket/Seal Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect gasket for cracks, deformation, or residue. Clean using approved methods and ensure it is evenly seated.

steps.title: Verify Proper Door Closing Technique
steps.instructions: Close the door firmly but not forcefully, ensuring even pressure and full seating before starting the cycle.

steps.title: Inspect Latch Mechanism Externally
steps.instructions: Observe for damage, looseness, or misalignment. Do not disassemble; lightly test latch movement if possible.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit (Sensor Reset)
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, wait 30 seconds, then power it back on and retry door closure.

steps.title: Check for Door Interlock or Sensor Errors
steps.instructions: Observe system messages indicating door open, door not locked, or interlock failure.

CCR.Complaint: AER door would not latch and cycle would not start.
CCR.Cause: Debris buildup on door gasket preventing proper seal and latch engagement.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned gasket and chamber lip, verified proper closure and successful cycle initiation.

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Endoscope Flushing System - No Flow or Insufficient Flushing Pressure
description: Addresses no flow or weak flushing pressure caused by water supply, tubing, filters, or connection issues affecting proper endoscope flushing.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is not in use on a scope intended for patient procedures. Remove from clinical workflow if flushing is required for reprocessing.

steps.title: Verify Water Supply to the Unit
steps.instructions: Confirm facility water supply is on, verify no upstream shutoff valves are closed, and observe for normal inlet pressure if gauge is present.

steps.title: Inspect Inlet Hose and Connections
steps.instructions: Straighten any kinks, tighten fittings securely, and look for leaks or damage.

steps.title: Check Internal and External Filters
steps.instructions: Remove and visually inspect filter screens and replace or clean filters if visibly dirty.

steps.title: Verify Proper Channel Hookup
steps.instructions: Confirm correct adapters are being used and ensure all connections are fully seated and not leaking.

steps.title: Test Flow Without Scope Connected
steps.instructions: Activate flushing cycle and observe output directly from ports. If flow is strong → issue likely with scope or adapters. If flow is weak or absent → continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect for Air in Lines or Priming Issues
steps.instructions: Run multiple flush cycles to purge air and observe for sputtering or inconsistent flow.

steps.title: Check for Blockage in Output Tubing or Ports
steps.instructions: Remove tubing and visually inspect and flush tubing separately if needed.

steps.title: Evaluate Facility Water Pressure
steps.instructions: Test pressure at the source and compare to manufacturer recommended range.

CCR.Complaint: Scope Buddy reported to have no flow during flushing cycle.
CCR.Cause: Clogged inlet filter restricting water flow.
CCR.Resolution: Filter cleaned/replaced and flow restored to normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Accessories or tubing swapped during troubleshooting
Power or system response during testing

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Endoscope Flushing System - Channel Not Flushing or Blockage Detected
description: Addresses failure to flush endoscope channels or blockage alarms caused by setup issues, tubing problems, or external flow restrictions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the scope is not in use on a patient before troubleshooting. Expected: Device is removed from clinical use. Why it matters: Prevents contamination risk or interruption of patient care.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to run a flush cycle and observe behavior. Action: Confirm which channel is not flushing; Note if there is no flow, reduced flow, or a blockage alarm; Observe for any error indicators or abnormal sounds. Expected: Issue is reproducible and clearly identified. Why it matters: Confirms a true flushing failure vs user setup issue.

steps.title: Check Water Supply Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Ensure the system has proper water supply. Action: Verify water source is turned ON; Check supply hose for kinks or disconnection; Confirm adequate pressure from the source. Expected: Strong, consistent water supply. Why it matters: Insufficient water supply will prevent proper flushing.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing and Channel Hookups
steps.instructions: Improper or obstructed tubing is a frequent cause. Action: Disconnect and reconnect all channel lines securely; Inspect tubing for kinks, cracks, or buildup; Ensure correct port-to-channel matching. Expected: Tubing is clear, properly routed, and securely connected. Why it matters: Misconnection or restriction directly prevents flow.

steps.title: Check for External Blockage in the Endoscope Channel
steps.instructions: The scope itself may be obstructed. Action: Disconnect the scope from the Scope Buddy; Attempt manual flushing using a syringe or alternate method; Observe resistance or inability to pass fluid. Expected: Fluid passes freely through the channel. Why it matters: Confirms whether blockage is in the scope vs the flushing system.

steps.title: Inspect Filters and Inline Components
steps.instructions: Filters can restrict flow if clogged. Action: Check inline filters for discoloration or debris; Replace filter if visibly dirty or overdue; Verify no inline check valves are stuck. Expected: Clean filter allowing unrestricted flow. Why it matters: Blocked filters reduce or stop flow to channels.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Tubing or Channel Port
steps.instructions: Isolate whether the issue is specific to one line. Action: Move tubing to a different port; Use a known-good tubing set if available. Expected: Normal flow resumes on alternate setup. Why it matters: Helps isolate a faulty port or tubing set.

steps.title: Observe for Pump Operation (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Some units use assisted flow mechanisms. Action: Listen for pump engagement during flush; Note abnormal sounds (grinding, no sound, intermittent operation). Expected: Pump runs smoothly during flushing. Why it matters: Lack of pump operation indicates internal failure.

CCR.Complaint: Scope Buddy not flushing channels; blockage alarm during use.
CCR.Cause: Kinked tubing restricting flow to channel port.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced tubing and verified normal flushing operation.

helpfulDetails: Any alarms or indicator lights noted
Final device status returned to service or removed

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Endoscope Flushing System - Pump Not Activating or Inconsistent Flow
description: Pump not running or inconsistent flushing flow due to power, tubing setup, fluid supply, or blockage issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while a scope is in use on a patient. Remove the scope and use an alternate flushing method if needed.

steps.title: Verify Power to the Unit
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit is plugged in and powered on. Check outlet and power cord. Ensure power switch is ON. Test outlet with another device. Inspect power cord for damage.

steps.title: Confirm Pump Activation Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt to activate the pump using the control interface. Press start/activate control. Listen for pump motor engagement. Observe for any indicator lights or alarms.

steps.title: Check Fluid Supply Source
steps.instructions: Ensure adequate fluid is available and properly connected. Verify fluid container is not empty. Confirm intake tubing is fully submerged. Check for kinks or disconnections.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Check all tubing for restrictions or improper setup. Verify tubing is correctly routed. Look for kinks, twists, or compression points. Reseat all quick-connect fittings.

steps.title: Check for Air in the System (Prime Issue)
steps.instructions: Air in the line can prevent proper pump operation. Prime the system per normal operation. Observe for air bubbles in tubing.

steps.title: Inspect Filters or Inline Components (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Some setups include filters or check valves. Inspect for clogging or saturation. Replace if visibly obstructed.

steps.title: Evaluate for Blockage at Output/Scope Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect the scope and test flow directly. Run pump with output disconnected from scope. Observe flow rate.

steps.title: Listen for Abnormal Pump Behavior
steps.instructions: Mechanical issues may present as sound changes. Listen for grinding, clicking, or silence when activated.

CCR.Complaint: Scope Buddy pump not activating and inconsistent flushing reported by staff.
CCR.Cause: Intake tubing partially kinked and air present in line causing intermittent flow.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected tubing routing, re-primed system, and verified consistent flow restored.

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Endoscope Flushing System - Leak or Connection Issues at Scope Interface
description: Leaks or poor connections at the scope interface causing fluid loss, poor flushing performance, or inability to maintain proper pressure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the scope from patient use and ensure it is not connected to a patient during troubleshooting. Confirm safe environment for equipment inspection.

steps.title: Inspect Scope Connection Setup
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the scope tubing. Ensure correct port matching (channel-to-channel). Confirm connections are fully seated.

steps.title: Check for Damaged or Worn Interface Fittings
steps.instructions: Look for cracks, worn O-rings, or deformation. Check for loose or stripped fittings.

steps.title: Verify Correct Adapter Usage
steps.instructions: Confirm adapter matches the scope model. Swap with a known-good adapter if available.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing and Lines for Leaks
steps.instructions: Look for cracks, kinks, or loose connections. Gently manipulate tubing while observing for leaks.

steps.title: Check for Proper Tightening (Do Not Overtighten)
steps.instructions: Hand-tighten fittings only. Avoid using tools unless specified.

steps.title: Run a Test Flush Cycle
steps.instructions: Operate the Scope Buddy with the scope connected. Observe the interface during operation. Look for active leaking or pressure drop.

steps.title: Swap with Known-Good Components
steps.instructions: Try a different scope. Try different tubing or adapters.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported leaking at the Scope Buddy interface during flushing cycle.
CCR.Cause: Worn O-ring on scope adapter causing poor seal under pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced adapter with known-good unit and verified no leaks during test cycle.

helpfulDetails: Adapter type and scope model used
Whether leak occurs immediately or under pressure

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Endoscope Flushing System - Power Failure or Control Panel Not Responding
description: Troubleshooting no power or unresponsive control panel issues on the Medivators Scope Buddy endoscope flushing system.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while a scope is connected for patient use. Remove the unit from service if needed. Expected: No risk to patient or contamination during troubleshooting. Why it matters: Prevents improper flushing or infection control issues.

steps.title: Verify Power Source (Most Common Issue)
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit is plugged into a known working outlet. Test outlet with another device or outlet tester. Check for GFCI reset if applicable. Expected: Unit powers on when proper power is supplied. Why it matters: Power loss is often external and easily corrected.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Check for loose, damaged, or frayed power cord. Ensure cord is fully seated in the unit and outlet. Gently move cord to check for intermittent power. Expected: Stable power with no flickering or loss when cord is secure. Why it matters: Damaged or loose cords can mimic internal failure.

steps.title: Check Power Switch and Indicators
steps.instructions: Confirm power switch is fully engaged. Observe for any indicator lights, sounds, or brief startup activity. Expected: Indicators illuminate or system attempts startup. Why it matters: Helps determine if power is reaching internal components.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Reset
steps.instructions: Turn unit OFF. Unplug from power. Wait 30–60 seconds. Plug back in and power ON. Expected: Control panel resets and becomes responsive. Why it matters: Clears temporary control board or software faults.

steps.title: Evaluate Control Panel Response
steps.instructions: Press buttons and observe for any response. Check for partial functionality (lights but no input, or frozen display). Expected: Buttons respond and system allows normal operation. Why it matters: Differentiates between full power failure and interface/control issue.

steps.title: Check for Environmental or External Factors
steps.instructions: Inspect for moisture exposure or fluid intrusion near control panel. Check for signs of overheating, unusual smell, or residue. Expected: No signs of contamination or environmental damage. Why it matters: Fluid exposure is common in reprocessing areas and can affect electronics.

steps.title: Inspect Internal Fuses (If Externally Accessible Only)
steps.instructions: Check for accessible fuse holder (no disassembly). Replace with correct rated fuse if blown. Expected: Unit powers on after fuse replacement. Why it matters: Blown fuse may indicate surge or power issue.

CCR.Complaint: Scope Buddy would not power on and control panel was unresponsive.
CCR.Cause: Fault traced to failed internal power supply after external power verified.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and sent to vendor for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any signs of moisture or fluid exposure
Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Plus Endoscope Flushing System - Inconsistent or Low Flushing Pressure
description: Troubleshooting low or inconsistent flushing pressure affecting endoscope cleaning performance.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the scope is not in use and has been removed from any patient procedure.

steps.title: Verify Water Supply Connection and Pressure Source
steps.instructions: Check that the incoming water line is securely connected and fully open. Inspect facility water supply valve and ensure no kinks or restrictions in supply tubing.

steps.title: Inspect External Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Check all hoses from the unit to the scope interface. Look for kinks, compression, or loose fittings and ensure proper connection at all ports.

steps.title: Check for Leaks at Scope Interface or Ports
steps.instructions: Run a flush cycle and observe for dripping or spraying. Tighten connections if needed.

steps.title: Verify Proper Scope Connection and Channel Hookup
steps.instructions: Ensure all channels are connected correctly using the appropriate adapters. Confirm correct hookup configuration and check for loose or mismatched connectors.

steps.title: Inspect and Replace Filters (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Remove and inspect inline water filters for debris or clogging. Replace if dirty or restricted.

steps.title: Check for Partial Channel Blockage in Scope or Adapters
steps.instructions: Disconnect scope and run a flush test. Compare pressure with and without scope attached.

steps.title: Observe Pump Operation and Sound
steps.instructions: Listen for weak, inconsistent, or unusually quiet pump operation and watch for intermittent cycling.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Unit
steps.instructions: Turn unit off, wait 10–15 seconds, then restart and test flushing.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported weak and inconsistent flushing pressure during scope processing.
CCR.Cause: Clogged inline water filter restricting flow and reducing system pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced filter, verified stable pressure during flush cycle, and returned unit to service.

helpfulDetails: Pressure behavior with and without scope attached
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Plus Endoscope Flushing System - Channel Connection or Detection Errors
description: Troubleshooting channel detection errors, connection issues, and inconsistent flushing on the Scope Buddy Plus.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the scope is intended for patient use. Remove the scope from service and use an alternate device if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the unit, observe for channel detection errors or missing indicators, and note when the issue occurs.

steps.title: Check Channel Tubing Connections (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Disconnect and firmly reconnect all tubing, ensuring correct port-to-channel matching.

steps.title: Inspect Channel Adapters and Fittings
steps.instructions: Check O-rings and connectors for wear or damage and replace if needed. Ensure correct adapters are used.

steps.title: Verify Scope Compatibility and Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm correct scope model, proper channel connections, and that unused ports are capped if required.

steps.title: Check for Blockages or Kinks in Tubing
steps.instructions: Inspect tubing for kinks or blockage, flush if possible, and replace if necessary.

steps.title: Perform a System Reset (Power Cycle)
steps.instructions: Turn off the unit, disconnect power for 10–15 seconds, then restart and reconnect channels.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Scope or Setup
steps.instructions: Connect a known-good scope or setup and verify if channels are detected properly.

CCR.Complaint: Scope Buddy Plus not detecting connected channels during setup.
CCR.Cause: Loose channel tubing connection causing improper pressure sensing.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected tubing securely and verified proper channel detection.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights or error messages observed

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Plus Endoscope Flushing System - Pump Failure or Irregular Operation
description: Troubleshooting pump failure, irregular operation, or inconsistent flow in the Medivators Scope Buddy Plus.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while an endoscope is in clinical use. Remove the scope from service if flushing is incomplete or unreliable.

steps.title: Verify Power and System Status
steps.instructions: Check power cord connection and outlet, confirm power indicator lights are on, and restart the unit.

steps.title: Confirm Pump Activation During Operation
steps.instructions: Start a flushing cycle, listen for pump motor sound, observe flow, and check for intermittent behavior.

steps.title: Check Water Supply and Source Connections
steps.instructions: Verify water source is connected and on, inspect inlet tubing, and confirm filters are not clogged.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing and Channel Connections
steps.instructions: Check tubing for kinks or leaks, ensure proper connections, and replace suspect tubing.

steps.title: Check for Air in the System (Priming Issue)
steps.instructions: Run a priming cycle without a scope, observe flow, and ensure lines are fully filled.

steps.title: Listen for Abnormal Pump Noise or Heat
steps.instructions: Listen for unusual sounds and check for overheating from the pump area.

steps.title: Attempt Multiple Cycles to Confirm Consistency
steps.instructions: Run multiple cycles and observe whether the issue is consistent or intermittent.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported Scope Buddy Plus pump not running consistently during flushing cycles.
CCR.Cause: Internal pump motor failure suspected after verifying power, tubing, and water supply were normal.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and sent to biomed shop/vendor for pump evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Accessories or tubing swapped during troubleshooting
Indicator lights or system status messages
Final device status (in service vs out of service)

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Plus Endoscope Flushing System - Control Panel or Keypad Malfunction
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive buttons, incorrect inputs, or frozen interface on the control panel.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the system is actively connected to a scope in use. Disconnect and move to a backup flushing method if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt multiple button presses, observe intermittent response, and check for frozen or delayed input.

steps.title: Inspect for Surface Contamination or Damage (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Inspect for moisture, residue, or debris. Clean with approved wipes and dry thoroughly before retesting.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle (Reset Control Interface)
steps.instructions: Power off, disconnect AC power for 10–15 seconds, then reconnect and power on.

steps.title: Check Power Stability and Indicators
steps.instructions: Verify outlet, check indicator lights, and avoid shared high-load outlets.

steps.title: Test Full Keypad Functionality
steps.instructions: Press each button individually and note any that fail or respond incorrectly.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Check for cracks, worn buttons, lifted membrane, or chemical damage.

CCR.Complaint: Control panel unresponsive; unable to start flushing cycle.
CCR.Cause: Keypad membrane failure confirmed after cleaning and power cycle did not restore function.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service and sent for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service vs out of service)

title: Medivators Scope Buddy Plus Endoscope Flushing System - Power or Startup Failure
description: Device not powering on or failing to start due to power, connection, or external issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while a scope is connected for patient use. Remove the device from clinical workflow if needed.

steps.title: Verify Power Source (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Check that the unit is plugged into a known working outlet. Test the outlet with another device.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the power cord is fully seated in both the wall outlet and the device. Inspect for damage, cuts, or loose connections.

steps.title: Check Power Switch and Indicators
steps.instructions: Toggle the power switch OFF, wait 5–10 seconds, then turn it back ON. Observe for any indicator lights, sounds, or display activity.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit Breaker or Power Strip (If Used)
steps.instructions: If the unit is connected to a power strip or surge protector, ensure it is turned ON and not tripped. Bypass it if possible.

steps.title: Check for Blown Facility GFCI Outlet
steps.instructions: If plugged into a GFCI outlet, check if it has tripped and reset it if needed.

steps.title: Observe for Partial Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: Listen for internal fans or pumps and watch for incomplete initialization or frozen state.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Reset
steps.instructions: Disconnect the unit from power for 30–60 seconds, then reconnect and attempt startup again.

CCR.Complaint: Scope Buddy Plus would not power on; no lights or response when switched on.
CCR.Cause: Fault traced to failed internal power supply after verifying outlet and power cord were functional.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent to vendor for repair; replacement unit placed into service.

helpfulDetails: Use of power strip or GFCI outlet
Final device status (non-functional, sent for repair)
